case_laws.title: Mst. Asia Bibi v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Landmark acquittal in a blasphemy case. The Supreme Court acquitted the accused due to insufficient evidence and contradictory statements of prosecution witnesses. The court emphasized that the burden of proof lies on the prosecution and mere allegations without corroborating evidence cannot sustain a conviction under Section 295-C PPC.
case_laws.summary_ur: توہین رسالت کے مقدمے میں تاریخی بریت۔ سپریم کورٹ نے ناکافی شواہد اور استغاثہ کے گواہوں کے متضاد بیانات کی بنا پر ملزمہ کو بری کر دیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Blasphemy - Section 295-C PPC - Burden of proof on prosecution - Contradictory witness statements - Acquittal on benefit of doubt

case_laws.title: Muhammad Nawaz v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Case regarding the right to bail in non-bailable offenses. The Supreme Court held that bail is a right, not a privilege, and that the accused should not be kept in custody merely because charges are heinous. The court must consider the merits of the case and likelihood of conviction.
case_laws.summary_ur: ناقابل ضمانت جرائم میں ضمانت کے حق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔ سپریم کورٹ نے فیصلہ دیا کہ ضمانت حق ہے، مراعات نہیں۔
case_laws.headnotes: Bail - Non-bailable offenses - Right vs privilege - Section 497 CrPC - Considerations for grant of bail

case_laws.title: The State v. Ahmad Ali
case_laws.summary_en: Case concerning the evidentiary value of DNA evidence in criminal trials. The court held that DNA evidence, when properly collected and analyzed, is admissible and carries significant evidentiary weight under the Qanun-e-Shahadat Order 1984.
case_laws.summary_ur: فوجداری مقدمات میں ڈی این اے شواہد کی ثبوتی قدر سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: DNA Evidence - Admissibility - Qanun-e-Shahadat Order 1984 - Scientific evidence in criminal trials

case_laws.title: Ghulam Mustafa v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: The Supreme Court held that FIR delay requires plausible explanation. Unexplained delay in lodging FIR creates doubt about prosecution case. Motive is a double-edged weapon and false implication cannot be ruled out when motive remains unproven.
case_laws.summary_ur: سپریم کورٹ نے فیصلہ دیا کہ ایف آئی آر میں تاخیر کی معقول وضاحت ضروری ہے۔
case_laws.headnotes: FIR delay - Prosecution burden - Motive double-edged - Benefit of doubt - Section 154 CrPC

case_laws.title: Amir Khan v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Case on pre-arrest bail in drug-related offenses. The court held that even in narcotics cases, pre-arrest bail can be granted where prima facie case for arrest is not made out. The court must weigh the evidence and consider whether the accused would abscond or tamper with evidence.
case_laws.summary_ur: منشیات کے مقدمات میں قبل از گرفتاری ضمانت سے متعلق فیصلہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Pre-arrest bail - Narcotics - CNSA Section 9 - Prima facie case - Abscondence risk
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Control of Narcotic Substances Act", "Code of Criminal Procedure"]

case_laws.title: Rashid Ahmed v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: The Sindh High Court quashed FIR registered under Section 489-F PPC for dishonoured cheque holding that dishonour of a post-dated cheque given as security does not attract criminal liability. The cheque must have been issued for repayment of a loan or obligation.
case_laws.summary_ur: سندھ ہائی کورٹ نے دفعہ 489-ایف کے تحت ایف آئی آر کالعدم قرار دیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Cheque dishonour - Section 489-F PPC - Post-dated security cheque - Criminal liability - FIR quashment

case_laws.title: Muhammad Aslam v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court established that recovery of weapon alone is insufficient to sustain a murder conviction. Recovery must be corroborated by independent evidence linking the weapon to the crime. Empty shells matching the weapon and blood-stained earth are important corroborating factors.
case_laws.summary_ur: سپریم کورٹ نے قرار دیا کہ صرف ہتھیار کی برآمدگی قتل کی سزا برقرار رکھنے کے لیے ناکافی ہے۔
case_laws.headnotes: Murder - Recovery of weapon - Corroborating evidence - Empty shells - Forensic report - Section 302 PPC

case_laws.title: Sardar Ali v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Landmark judgment on the rights of accused during investigation. The court held that police cannot use coercion or torture to extract confessions. Any statement obtained through torture is inadmissible. Right against self-incrimination under Article 13(b) of the Constitution is absolute.
case_laws.summary_ur: تفتیش کے دوران ملزم کے حقوق پر تاریخی فیصلہ۔ پولیس اعتراف جرم حاصل کرنے کے لیے تشدد نہیں کر سکتی۔
case_laws.headnotes: Rights of accused - Police torture - Inadmissible confession - Article 13(b) Constitution - Self-incrimination
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Constitution of Pakistan 1973", "Code of Criminal Procedure", "Qanun-e-Shahadat Order"]

case_laws.title: Fazal Rahim v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Case on circumstantial evidence in murder trials. The court held that conviction can be based solely on circumstantial evidence provided the chain of circumstances is complete, consistent, and points only to the guilt of the accused. Last-seen evidence alone is not sufficient.
case_laws.summary_ur: قتل کے مقدمات میں حالاتی شواہد سے متعلق فیصلہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Circumstantial evidence - Murder - Complete chain - Last seen evidence - Section 302 PPC - Benefit of doubt

case_laws.title: Imran Shah v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: The court held that medical evidence contradicting ocular evidence creates a serious doubt in the prosecution case. Where medical evidence regarding the nature, number, and site of injuries is inconsistent with eyewitness testimony, the prosecution case becomes doubtful.
case_laws.summary_ur: عدالت نے فیصلہ دیا کہ عینی شہادت سے متضاد طبی شواہد استغاثہ کے مقدمے میں سنگین شک پیدا کرتے ہیں۔
case_laws.headnotes: Medical evidence - Ocular evidence - Contradiction - Prosecution case - Acquittal - Benefit of doubt

case_laws.title: Zahid Hussain v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Case concerning kidnapping for ransom under Section 365-A PPC. The court held that for conviction, prosecution must establish (i) abduction (ii) demand for ransom and (iii) identification of accused by victim. Recovery of ransom money is a strong corroborating factor.
case_laws.summary_ur: دفعہ 365-اے کے تحت تاوان کے لیے اغوا سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Kidnapping for ransom - Section 365-A PPC - Identification - Recovery of ransom - Corroboration

case_laws.title: Saeed Ahmad v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: The Lahore High Court ruled on the admissibility of CCTV footage as evidence. The court held that CCTV recordings are admissible under Article 164 QSO as electronic evidence, provided the chain of custody is established and the recording has not been tampered with.
case_laws.summary_ur: لاہور ہائی کورٹ نے سی سی ٹی وی فوٹیج کی بطور ثبوت قابلیت پر فیصلہ دیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: CCTV evidence - Electronic evidence - Chain of custody - Article 164 QSO - Admissibility

case_laws.title: Abdul Rehman v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: The Supreme Court clarified the scope of Section 345 CrPC on compounding of offenses. The court held that Qatl-i-Amd can be compounded by heirs of the victim through a compromise (Sulh), but the court retains discretion to refuse compromise if it appears to be under duress or against public interest.
case_laws.summary_ur: سپریم کورٹ نے دفعہ 345 ضابطہ فوجداری کے تحت جرائم کی مصالحت کا دائرہ واضح کیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Compounding offenses - Section 345 CrPC - Qatl-i-Amd - Sulh compromise - Court's discretion - Public interest

case_laws.title: Noor Muhammad v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Case regarding the evidentiary value of dying declaration. The court held that a dying declaration must be voluntary, truthful, and made when the declarant was in a fit state of mind. If the dying declaration is corroborated by other evidence, it can form the sole basis of conviction.
case_laws.summary_ur: مرنے سے پہلے بیان کی ثبوتی قدر سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Dying declaration - Evidentiary value - Voluntary statement - Fit state of mind - Corroboration - Section 46 QSO

case_laws.title: Faisal Mehmood v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: The Supreme Court discussed the scope and applicability of Section 34 PPC on common intention. The court held that to attract Section 34, the prosecution must establish a pre-arranged plan or meeting of minds between the accused to commit the criminal act. Mere presence at the scene is insufficient.
case_laws.summary_ur: سپریم کورٹ نے دفعہ 34 مجموعہ تعزیرات پاکستان کے دائرہ کار پر تبادلہ خیال کیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Common intention - Section 34 PPC - Pre-arranged plan - Meeting of minds - Mere presence insufficient

case_laws.title: Naveed Akhtar v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the rights of juveniles in the criminal justice system under JJSA 2018. The court held that every juvenile is entitled to legal representation, family notification upon arrest, and trial by a Juvenile Court. Detention of juveniles with adult offenders is prohibited.
case_laws.summary_ur: جوائنل جسٹس سسٹم ایکٹ 2018 کے تحت نوجوانوں کے حقوق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Juvenile justice - JJSA 2018 - Legal representation - Family notification - Juvenile Court - Separate detention
case_laws.relevant_statutes: ["Juvenile Justice System Act", "Code of Criminal Procedure"]

case_laws.title: Tahir Mehmood v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: The Lahore High Court ruled on the legality of identification parade under Section 21-A CrPC. The court emphasized that identification parade must be conducted by a Magistrate, suspects must be mixed with similar-looking persons, and the witness must not have seen the accused before the parade.
case_laws.summary_ur: لاہور ہائی کورٹ نے شناختی پریڈ کی قانونی حیثیت پر فیصلہ دیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Identification parade - Section 21-A CrPC - Magistrate supervision - Fair procedure - Similar persons

case_laws.title: Babar Ali v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the rights of accused during police investigation. The court held that police remand beyond 14 days is illegal under Article 10 of the Constitution. The accused must be produced before a Magistrate every 15 days and has the right to consult a legal practitioner.
case_laws.summary_ur: پولیس تفتیش کے دوران ملزم کے حقوق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Police remand - 14 days limit - Article 10 Constitution - Right to counsel - Magistrate production

case_laws.title: Khalid Hussain v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court ruled that extra-judicial confession made before private persons is weak evidence. Such confession is only admissible if it is voluntary, reliable, and made in circumstances suggesting its truthfulness. Extra-judicial confession alone cannot sustain conviction.
case_laws.summary_ur: سپریم کورٹ نے فیصلہ دیا کہ نجی افراد کے سامنے عدالت سے باہر اعتراف جرم کمزور شہادت ہے۔
case_laws.headnotes: Extra-judicial confession - Weak evidence - Voluntary statement - Reliability - Private persons - Cannot sustain conviction alone

case_laws.title: Shah Nawaz v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: The Balochistan High Court addressed honour killing under Section 311 PPC. The court held that fasad-fil-arz provision allows the State to prosecute even after compromise between heirs. Honor killing in the name of ghairat constitutes fasad-fil-arz and the court may refuse to accept compromise.
case_laws.summary_ur: بلوچستان ہائی کورٹ نے دفعہ 311 کے تحت غیرت کے نام پر قتل پر فیصلہ دیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Honour killing - Fasad-fil-arz - Section 311 PPC - State prosecution - Compromise refusal - Ghairat

case_laws.title: Arshad Mehmood v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Case on anti-terrorism court jurisdiction. The Lahore High Court held that for an offense to be triable by Anti-Terrorism Court, the act must create terror in the public, not merely cause private harm. Personal enmity-based crimes generally do not attract ATA jurisdiction.
case_laws.summary_ur: انسداد دہشت گردی عدالت کے دائرہ اختیار سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: ATA jurisdiction - Terror in public - Private enmity - Section 6 ATA - Transfer to ordinary court

case_laws.title: Muhammad Bilal v. The State
case_laws.judge_name: Justice Sayyed Mazahar Ali Akbar Naqvi
case_laws.summary_en: The Supreme Court addressed the evidentiary value of chance witnesses. The court held that testimony of a chance witness must be scrutinized with greater care. The witness must explain his presence at the crime scene, and his testimony must be corroborated by independent evidence.
case_laws.summary_ur: سپریم کورٹ نے اتفاقی گواہوں کی شہادت کی ثبوتی قدر پر فیصلہ دیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Chance witness - Scrutiny required - Presence at scene - Corroboration needed - Evidentiary value

case_laws.title: Imdad Ali v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court landmark judgment on mental illness and criminal responsibility. The court held that a person suffering from schizophrenia at the time of commission of offense may be entitled to the defense of insanity under Section 84 PPC. Psychiatric evaluation by a qualified professional is essential.
case_laws.summary_ur: ذہنی بیماری اور فوجداری ذمہ داری پر سپریم کورٹ کا تاریخی فیصلہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Insanity defense - Section 84 PPC - Schizophrenia - Psychiatric evaluation - Criminal responsibility - McNaghten Rule

case_laws.title: Sajjad Hussain v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: The Peshawar High Court discussed the right of private defense under Section 100 PPC. The court held that the right of private defense extends to causing death if there is reasonable apprehension of death or grievous hurt. The response must be proportionate to the threat.
case_laws.summary_ur: پشاور ہائی کورٹ نے دفعہ 100 کے تحت ذاتی دفاع کے حق پر تبادلہ خیال کیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Private defense - Section 100 PPC - Right to cause death - Reasonable apprehension - Proportionate response

case_laws.title: Farhan Ahmed v. NAB
case_laws.summary_en: Case concerning the powers and limitations of NAB under the National Accountability Ordinance 1999. The court held that NAB cannot arrest an accused merely on suspicion; there must be reasonable grounds supported by material evidence. The accused has the right to bail if the case is based on weak evidence.
case_laws.summary_ur: نیب کے اختیارات اور حدود سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: NAB powers - NAO 1999 - Arrest on suspicion - Reasonable grounds - Bail in accountability cases

case_laws.title: Mst. Kausar Parveen v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court case on acid attack under Section 336-B PPC. The court upheld severe punishment and held that acid attacks constitute heinous crimes against women. Compensation to the victim for reconstructive surgery and rehabilitation is mandatory.
case_laws.summary_ur: تیزاب حملے سے متعلق سپریم کورٹ کا مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Acid attack - Section 336-B PPC - Heinous crime - Mandatory compensation - Reconstructive surgery

case_laws.title: Muhammad Rizwan v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: The Lahore High Court addressed the issue of delay in trial. The court held that speedy trial is a fundamental right under Article 10-A of the Constitution. Prolonged incarceration without trial violates due process, and the accused may be entitled to bail on the ground of delay alone.
case_laws.summary_ur: لاہور ہائی کورٹ نے مقدمے میں تاخیر کے مسئلے پر فیصلہ دیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Speedy trial - Article 10-A Constitution - Right to fair trial - Prolonged incarceration - Bail for delay

case_laws.title: Ali Hassan v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: The Supreme Court addressed cross-examination of prosecution witnesses. The court held that the right to cross-examine is fundamental to fair trial. Denial of cross-examination renders the witness testimony inadmissible. The court must ensure that the accused is afforded full opportunity to cross-examine.
case_laws.summary_ur: سپریم کورٹ نے استغاثہ کے گواہوں کی جرح پر فیصلہ دیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Cross-examination - Fair trial - Fundamental right - Denial renders testimony inadmissible - Article 10-A

case_laws.title: Waqas Ahmed v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Case on the application of Section 10 QSO (conspiracy evidence). The court held that acts and declarations of co-conspirators are admissible against each other only if there is reasonable ground to believe a conspiracy exists. Independent evidence of conspiracy must be established first.
case_laws.summary_ur: دفعہ 10 قانون شہادت (سازش کے شواہد) کے اطلاق سے متعلق مقدمہ۔
case_laws.headnotes: Conspiracy - Section 10 QSO - Co-conspirator statements - Independent evidence - Reasonable ground

case_laws.title: Asad Ullah v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: Supreme Court case addressing digital forensic evidence. The court held that WhatsApp messages, call records, and digital communications are admissible as evidence under PECA and QSO, provided they are obtained through lawful means and authenticated through proper forensic analysis.
case_laws.summary_ur: سپریم کورٹ نے ڈیجیٹل فرانزک شواہد پر فیصلہ دیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Digital evidence - WhatsApp messages - Call records - PECA - QSO - Forensic authentication - Lawful means

case_laws.title: Hamza Ali v. The State
case_laws.summary_en: The Sindh High Court addressed the issue of double jeopardy under Article 13(a) of the Constitution. The court held that no person can be punished twice for the same offense. If an accused has been acquitted or convicted by a competent court, he cannot be tried again for the same offense.
case_laws.summary_ur: سندھ ہائی کورٹ نے آئین کی دفعہ 13 کے تحت دوہری سزا کے مسئلے پر فیصلہ دیا۔
case_laws.headnotes: Double jeopardy - Article 13(a) Constitution - Same offense - Previous acquittal/conviction - Competent court
